facts: Guest arrived late with a confirmed Sheraton reservation and was told the property was oversold with no room available.
requested_compensation: Comparable nearby hotel
Transportation to alternate hotel
Cash or points under the Ultimate Reservation Guarantee
actual_outcome: Hotel arranged a nearby property, reimbursed rideshare, and provided points after corporate follow-up.
reusable_lesson: Ask the property to document that it could not honor a confirmed reservation, then reference the brand guarantee in writing.
notes: Synthetic example for the first demo.

facts: A crew-related delay caused an overnight misconnect. Passenger requested hotel, meal, and transportation support.
requested_compensation: Hotel voucher
Meal voucher
Ground transportation
Rebooking on the next available flight
actual_outcome: Airline provided hotel and meal vouchers at the airport service desk.
reusable_lesson: For controllable disruptions, use the airline's DOT dashboard commitments and ask for care before paying out of pocket.
notes: Synthetic example for the first demo.

facts: Passenger arrived more than three hours late on a qualifying EU itinerary and requested fixed compensation plus care.
requested_compensation: EU261 fixed compensation if eligible
Meals and care expenses
Written explanation for the delay reason
actual_outcome: Passenger received a written denial first, then compensation after providing itinerary and arrival-time evidence.
reusable_lesson: Anchor the request on actual arrival delay, route eligibility, and whether extraordinary circumstances apply.
notes: Synthetic example for the first demo.

facts: Passenger was required to gate-check a bag on a Southwest itinerary with a connection through MDW. Staff said the bag would continue to SEA, but it remained at MDW and was expected the next day. The passenger had important keys in the bag and was offered either airport pickup credit or home delivery without compensation.
requested_compensation: Baggage delay claim review
Reimbursement for necessary replacement items
Credit or compensation for pickup inconvenience
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported in the captured thread. Other users suggested filing with Southwest claims and keeping receipts for necessary purchases.
escalation_path: Credit card travel protection if eligible
reusable_lesson: For gate-checked baggage that does not arrive, file the baggage report immediately, keep the claim number, and document necessary expenses instead of relying only on verbal airport offers.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum '怎么撕？' tagged thread. Verify source details before public launch.

facts: Passenger flew from Paris Orly to Madrid on Iberia business class with six checked bags. After arrival, staff said the aircraft's checked baggage had not been loaded and the passenger filed a standard delayed baggage report.
requested_compensation: Reimbursement for reasonable emergency purchases
Clarification of Montreal Convention baggage liability
Coordination with credit card baggage delay coverage
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported in the captured thread. Forum advice emphasized claiming with the final operating carrier first and using card insurance only for eligible remaining expenses.
reusable_lesson: Delayed baggage claims usually reimburse reasonable necessary purchases rather than paying an automatic lump sum; keep receipts and clarify whether airline reimbursement affects card coverage.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum '怎么撕？' tagged thread. Verify legal limits and current carrier rules before public launch.

facts: A weather-related first-segment delay made a same-day connection impractical. The passenger proactively searched alternate routing, requested rebooking, incurred hotel, food, rental car, and fuel expenses, then pursued both airline goodwill and Amex Platinum trip delay insurance.
requested_compensation: Rebooking on workable alternate routing
Airline goodwill miles or voucher
Trip delay insurance reimbursement
actual_outcome: Passenger reported airline goodwill of miles and a voucher, plus a $500 trip delay insurance reimbursement after submitting supporting documents.
reusable_lesson: Search alternate routes before contacting the airline, ask for the new ticket number after rebooking, and obtain a written delay reason early for trip insurance.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum travel DP. This is a travel insurance and goodwill case, not a controllable-delay rights case.

facts: Guest booked a Holiday Inn in Boston through Chase Sapphire Preferred and later noticed a security deposit charge that appeared incorrect. The guest considered a card dispute before contacting the hotel.
requested_compensation: Refund of incorrect security deposit charge
Written folio or explanation of the charge
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported beyond forum advice. Other users recommended contacting or emailing the hotel first because the charge might be a billing error or pending adjustment.
reusable_lesson: For unexplained hotel charges, request the folio and a written billing correction from the property before escalating to a card dispute.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum '怎么撕？' tagged thread.

facts: Guest at a Hampton property ordered food through a QR code promoted in the hotel area, paid including tip, then learned the restaurant was closed and the order did not arrive. The front desk said it was unrelated to the hotel.
requested_compensation: Refund from restaurant or payment processor
Hotel goodwill points for poor on-property communication
Clarification whether the restaurant was hotel-affiliated
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported in the captured thread. Forum advice leaned toward disputing the undelivered food charge with the merchant/payment card and treating hotel compensation as goodwill only.
reusable_lesson: Separate the refund claim against the food merchant from any goodwill request to the hotel, and document whether the restaurant was clearly presented as a hotel amenity.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum '怎么撕？' tagged thread.

facts: Guest brought personal towels to a Hyatt stay and housekeeping appears to have removed them with hotel linens. The front desk initially offered a hotel towel rather than finding or reimbursing the personal item.
requested_compensation: Return of personal item if recoverable
Reimbursement based on item value
Goodwill points
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported in the captured thread. Forum advice suggested deciding the desired remedy first, then asking the manager or Hyatt customer care for item replacement or points.
evidence_used: Description or receipt for personal item
escalation_path: Credit card purchase protection if eligible
reusable_lesson: For minor personal-property loss at a hotel, anchor the request on replacement value and documentation, while treating points or fee adjustment as goodwill.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum '怎么撕？' tagged thread.

source_name: USCardForum thread summary via Nitan MCP
facts: Passenger on an American Airlines connecting itinerary to Paris said check-in was delayed because the airline system did not recognize a Schengen visa. Manual verification was completed very close to departure, leaving no time to check a bag. The passenger boarded without the checked bag and expected to buy personal items after arrival.
requested_compensation: Written explanation that check-in delay was caused by airline document-verification issue
Reimbursement or goodwill for necessary replacement items
Customer relations review
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported in the captured thread. Forum replies noted that because the bag was never accepted and no baggage report existed, normal delayed-baggage rules would be hard to invoke, though a goodwill request could still be attempted.
evidence_used: Names or notes from check-in staff if available
Receipts for necessary purchases at destination
escalation_path: Airport check-in counter if still on site
Employer travel reimbursement if business travel
reusable_lesson: If airline check-in processing prevents baggage acceptance, ask for written documentation immediately; without a bag tag or property irregularity report, the claim is weaker and should be framed as a customer-service failure rather than a standard baggage-delay claim.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum topic read via Nitan MCP. Verify details before public launch.

source_name: USCardForum thread summary via Nitan MCP
facts: Passenger booked a Cathay Pacific itinerary through Chase Travel. The original Cathay flight was delayed for mechanical reasons and then cancelled overnight. Cathay rebooked the passenger onto a United flight, which was also delayed for mechanical reasons after boarding, creating uncertainty over which carrier should handle support.
requested_compensation: Clear operating-carrier responsibility for current delay support
Rebooking or rerouting if delay becomes extended
Meal or hotel support if overnight
Written delay and cancellation reasons from each carrier
actual_outcome: Outcome not reported in the captured thread. Forum replies were split, with several suggesting the passenger continue with Cathay for the original disruption while also asking United for support on the operating flight it controlled.
escalation_path: Operating carrier airport desk for immediate care
Cathay customer service for original cancellation handling
Chase Travel if ticket control or reissue problems arise
DOT complaint only after documenting denied commitments
reusable_lesson: For mixed-carrier irregular operations, separate the original carrier's cancellation responsibility from the operating carrier's current delay support, and collect written reasons from both before making reimbursement or goodwill requests.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum topic read via Nitan MCP.

source_name: USCardForum thread summary via Nitan MCP
facts: A new Hyatt Regency property in Rome repeatedly pushed back its opening date and began relocating some guests to a Hilton Garden Inn during peak-season dates. Forum discussion focused on whether the substitute property was comparable and whether guests should proactively contact Hyatt or wait for formal relocation instructions.
requested_compensation: Comparable replacement hotel
Written confirmation of relocated booking
Return of points or certificates if the substitute is lower value
Goodwill points if relocation is materially worse
actual_outcome: Outcome varied or was not fully reported in the captured thread. One discussion point was that Hyatt might only notify guests after a relocation arrangement was ready.
evidence_used: Screenshots of cash rates and category/value differences
reusable_lesson: For pre-arrival hotel relocation caused by delayed opening, ask for a written comparable-accommodation plan and preserve evidence of the original booking value before accepting a lower-tier substitute.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum topic read via Nitan MCP.

source_name: USCardForum thread summary via Nitan MCP
facts: Guest used points and suite upgrade benefits at Andaz 5th Avenue, then paid a nightly upgrade charge for a Wellness Suite mainly for the bathtub. The room allegedly lacked or had broken advertised amenities such as certain in-room equipment. Engineering could not fix one feature, and a promised points adjustment reportedly appeared incorrectly later.
requested_compensation: Refund or partial refund of paid upgrade charge
Points compensation for broken or missing advertised amenities
Correction of incorrect points adjustment
Manager review
actual_outcome: Partial outcome was discussed but not fully resolved in the captured thread. The thread reported an initial points promise and later confusion over the posted amount.
evidence_used: Room type description or website screenshots
Photos of missing or broken amenities
reusable_lesson: When paying for a specific room feature, document the advertised feature, the paid upgrade amount, and the defect while still on property; if points are promised, ask for the amount and case note in writing.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum topic read via Nitan MCP.

source_name: USCardForum thread summary via Nitan MCP
facts: Hyatt Regency Maui notified guests that the Regency Club would be closed during a renovation period. The property offered daily breakfast at another restaurant for Globalists and options for guests using club-related awards, such as keeping breakfast access or having an award returned.
requested_compensation: Daily breakfast for registered guests
Return of club access or guest of honor award if preferred
Possible goodwill if evening club value was important
actual_outcome: Forum consensus in the captured replies was that restaurant breakfast was a reasonable or even favorable substitute for this property, while additional compensation beyond the stated options was uncertain.
evidence_used: Reservation showing club access or elite status
Terms of the award or elite benefit
reusable_lesson: For temporary club lounge closures, compare the substitute breakfast and award-return options against the lost benefit; stronger asks need evidence that the closure materially reduced the booked benefit.
notes: Summarized and rewritten from a USCardForum topic read via Nitan MCP.
